frameworks.name: Bayesian observer model
frameworks.one_line: Posterior estimation combining slow-speed prior with sensory likelihood

frameworks.name: efficient coding
frameworks.one_line: Constraint linking sensory uncertainty structure to stimulus statistics

frameworks.name: neural population coding
frameworks.one_line: Quantifying MT population accuracy as a 'neural prior' over speed

core_question: Do MT neurons' speed tuning characteristics quantitatively match the prior inferred from human psychophysics under an efficient-coding-constrained Bayesian model?
key_assumptions: Visual speed perception follows Bayesian inference
Sensory uncertainty is structured by efficient coding
Slow-speed prior follows a power-law form
Population coding accuracy can be read out as a neural prior
manipulations: Fit Bayesian observer model to psychophysical speed data
Extract behavioral prior from model fit
Estimate neural prior from MT population coding accuracy
key_findings: Bayesian observer model with efficient coding explains human visual speed perception
MT population coding accuracy is precisely predicted by the behavioral slow-speed prior
Behavioral prior and neural prior are indistinguishable under cross-validation
interpretations: Bayesian observer model constrained by efficient coding is quantitatively validated at both behavioral and neural levels
Slow-speed prior in perception has a direct correspondence in MT population tuning
